case_title: ROY WILLIS et al VS. DUANE HARPER et al

docket_entries.description: DEMAND FOR JURY AND MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE FILED BY DEFENDANT HARPER, DUANE

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEAN WILLIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. PLAINTIFF SUSTAINED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. LATE REPLY PAPERS WERE SUBMITTED TO THE COURT BY THE PLAINTIFF FOR THE COURTS REVIEW. AFTER REVIEWING THE REPLY PAPERS THE COURT DETERMINED THAT THE MOTION SHOULD BE GRANTED. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER: JOYCE LONG, CSR # 8620
DECLARATION OF DAILY RENTAL VALUE $83.33 PER DAY FILED BY PLAINTIFF WILLIS, ROY WILLIS, DEAN

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF WILLIS, ROY WILLIS, DEAN RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT HARPER, DUANE THE RESTITUTION AND POSSESSION OF THE PREMISES: 128 MORRIS STREET, #11, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94107, LEASE FORFEITED, UNPAID RENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $5,000.00; AND UNLAWFUL DETAINER DAMAGES IN THE RATE OF $83.33 FROM AUGUST 1, 2009, THROUGH ENTRY OF JUDGMENT IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,916.55. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT FOR FURTHER DETAILS OF THE JUDGMENT

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
John Zanghi, Esq of Zanghi, Torres & Arshawsky, 415-977-0444 for plaintiff and moving party
Duane Lucas-Harper, , 415-971-4870 defendant and opposing party appearing propria persona

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND GRANTED PLAINTIFF ROY WILLIS, DEAN WILLIS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT. PLAINTIFF SUSTAINED THE BURDEN OF PROOF. LATE REPLY PAPERS WERE SUBMITTED TO THE COURT BY THE PLAINTIFF FOR THE COURTS REVIEW. AFTER REVIEWING THE REPLY PAPERS THE COURT DETERMINED THAT THE MOTION SHOULD BE GRANTED. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Reporter: Joyce Long, CSR # 8620 (301/HEK)
